semanticCore: Sandals, shoes, protective foot wear, shoeing animals, or flat hard ground

keyDerivations.meaning: Sandals, shoes, or protective footwear worn on the feet

figurativeOrRareUsages: إِنِّي أَنَا رَبُّكَ فَاخْلَعْ نَعْلَيْكَ ۖ إِنَّكَ بِالْوَادِ الْمُقَدَّسِ طُوًى (20:12): 'Indeed, I am your Lord, so remove your sandals/shoes; indeed, you are in the sacred valley of Tuwa.'
summaryEn: The root represents footwear or sandals (Na'l), ordering Moses to remove his shoes to show respect for the holy ground of the valley of Tuwa.
summaryAr: يشير هذا الجذر إلى النعلين الحذاء والمداس الواقي للقدم، والأرض الصلبة (نعل)، آمراً موسى بخلع نعليه احتراماً وتقديساً للوادي المقدس طوى.
rawText: أَنْعَلْتُ الخُفَّ 4 and 🡻نَعَّلْتُهُ I affixed a sole to the bottom of the خَفّ [i. e. boot]: and hence, أَنْعَلْتُ الدَّابَّهَ and 🡻نَعَلْتُهَا. (Msb.) See صِرْمٌ. اِنْتَعَلَتْ ظِلاَلَهَا 8 : see ظِلٌّ. نَاعِلٌ Wearing, or having on the feet, sandals. نَعَّلَ 2 see 4. نَعْلٌ [A sandal: a sole:] the thing by which the foot is preserved, or protected, from the ground; (K;) syn. حِذَآءٌ: and also applied to a تَاسُومَة [or shoe]. (Msb.) What is now called تَاسُومَة. (IAth, TA.) It often signifies only a sole: so in the S, K, Msb, &c., in art. خصف &c. === The leathern shoe, or sandal, of a camel; which is attached by thongs, or straps, called سَرَائِح (pl. of سَرِيحَةٌ) to the خَدَمَة or plaited thong which surrounds the pastern: see سَرِيحَةٌ and خَدَنَةٌ. === نَعْلٌ of a sword The iron, (Kr, S, K,) or silver, (S,) thing [or shoe] at the lower end of the scabbard. (Kr, S, K.) See غَاشِيَةٌ, and 2 in art. فرص. === نَعْلٌ meaning A حَرَّة, or hard rugged tract of land, &c.: see رَحْلٌ. === نَعْلٌ ‡ A wife. See عَتَبَة. نَعڤلَ 1 see 4. نِعَالِيٌّ One who takes care of the sandals or shoes [at the door of a bath or mosque]. (TA in art. ثوب.)
